sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(2) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland streams, rivers, and canals)
sources.quotedText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions. From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish. In waters where the bag limit for trout is zero, trout must be released unharmed and should not be removed from the water.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(152) — Tahoe Lake and tributaries (checked and found NOT to apply: Phipps Creek is not a Lake Tahoe tributary — it drains west into the Rubicon River / Middle Fork American River, not into Lake Tahoe)
sources.quotedText: (152) Tahoe Lake and tributaries (Placer and El Dorado Cos.). (A) Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout. (B) Tahoe Lake within 300 feet of the mouth of its tributaries. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout.

sources.title: OpenStreetMap — Phipps Creek (way 435243727): a west/southwest-flowing headwaters stream below Phipps Lake in the Desolation Wilderness that joins the Rubicon River near 38.9647, -120.2035 (Middle Fork American River drainage — not a Lake Tahoe tributary)
Rubicon River (California) — Wikipedia (establishes the receiving river drains to the American River, west of Lake Tahoe)
sources.quotedText: The Rubicon River is a major tributary of the Middle Fork American River in the Sierra Nevada of Northern California, west of Lake Tahoe.

sources.quotedText: Permits are required year-round for overnight use.

sources.title: Desolation Day Use Permits & Parking — Eldorado National Forest, USDA Forest Service
sources.quotedText: Day use is not subject to fees nor limited by quota at any time of the year.

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams (Mouth) layer record for gaz_id 230601, "Phipps Creek", Stream, El Dorado Co., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":230601,"gaz_name":"Phipps Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"El Dorado"} — geometry multipoint; first point (mouth) converts to lon -120.203258, lat 38.964635, matching this file's own OpenStreetMap source's stated confluence with the Rubicon River near 38.9647, -120.2035; correcting the file's prior coordinate (-120.1523, 38.96047), which was actually a headwaters/source-area point (the record's second multipoint vertex, lon -120.14668, lat 38.96167), ~4.4km from the true mouth.

end.verbatim: the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April

parameters.note: Tahoe-tributary check (required for Tahoe-basin waters): Phipps Creek is a Desolation Wilderness headwaters stream (El Dorado Co.) that flows WEST/southwest out of the Phipps Lake area and joins the Rubicon River (confluence traced in OpenStreetMap at ~38.9647, -120.2035) — it drains to the Middle Fork American River, NOT into Lake Tahoe. It is therefore NOT a Lake Tahoe tributary, so the §7.50(b)(152) 'Tahoe Lake tributaries' special catch-and-release closure does NOT apply. Phipps Creek is also not listed by its own name in §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide stream regulation §5.85(a)(2): open with a five-trout bag from the last Saturday in April through November 15, then a zero-trout catch-and-release period with gear restrictions from November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April.

regulations.humanSummary: Open the last Saturday in April through November 15 (general statewide stream regulation — not a Lake Tahoe tributary, so the §7.50(b)(152) Tahoe closure does not apply); November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April is a zero-trout, artificial-lures-with-barbless-hooks period
regulations.verbatimText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions. From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish. In waters where the bag limit for trout is zero, trout must be released unharmed and should not be removed from the water.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland streams, rivers, and canals: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, in effect from the last Saturday in April through November 15.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit, last Saturday in April through November 15
regulations.verbatimText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions.

parameters.counting_rule: Zero-trout bag limit from November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April; trout must be released unharmed and should not be removed from the water.

regulations.humanSummary: Zero-trout bag limit (catch-and-release for trout), November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April
regulations.verbatimText: From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish. In waters where the bag limit for trout is zero, trout must be released unharmed and should not be removed from the water.

parameters.note: Gear restriction applies from November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, for all species of fish. During the regular season (last Saturday in April through November 15) the general stream regulation imposes no gear restrictions.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April
regulations.verbatimText: From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. Phipps Creek is wholly within California (El Dorado Co.); no Nevada reciprocity applies.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.description: Phipps Creek lies within the Desolation Wilderness (Eldorado National Forest / Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit; confirmed inside the Desolation Wilderness protected-area boundary). A wilderness permit is required to enter: a free day-use permit (self-issued at major trailheads, no fee and not quota-limited) and, for overnight trips, an overnight permit required year-round. Overnight use is governed by a zone-quota system in effect from the Friday before Memorial Day through September 30 each year; overnight permits and reservations are handled through Recreation.gov (permit #233261).

raw.day_use_permit: required; free; no fee; not quota-limited
raw.overnight_quota_season: Friday before Memorial Day through September 30

regulations.citation: USDA Forest Service — Desolation Wilderness Permits (Eldorado National Forest)
regulations.humanSummary: In Desolation Wilderness: a free day-use permit and (for overnight trips) an overnight permit required year-round; overnight zone-quota season runs the Friday before Memorial Day through September 30 (reserve via Recreation.gov)
regulations.verbatimText: Permits are required year-round for overnight use.
